name: housing_clinical_trial_amendment_would_stonehaven_lib_eq
premise: A Housing response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving housing clinical trial amendment would stonehaven lib eq. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as liberty vs equality under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Hector Chandra
agents.identity: I am Hector Chandra, accountable for a defensible call in Housing when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays equality for liberty.

agents.name: Talia Patel
agents.identity: I am Talia Patel, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Housing.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so equality isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of liberty.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect equality.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Hector Chandra must decide under liberty vs equality. What does Hector Chandra do?
